conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I have 1 unit of X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE is offering ZUP to buy resources. I should start with an asking price slightly above my cost to allow room for negotiation, but not so high that it discourages a trade immediately. Let's aim for 45 ZUP as the first proposal. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to make. I am willing to sell it to you for 45 ZUP. Let's see if this works for you. </message>

<other player message> 
Oh, so you made this *X* for 40 ZUP? That’s adorable. I see you’re already trying to squeeze 5 extra ZUP out of me—how sweet. But 42 is a *much* more reasonable price for your *trash*. If that’s the best you can do, you’re either a fool or just eager to unload your scrap. Take it or leave it, but don’t expect me to pay your inflated "cost." Your 40 ZUP is already a generous discount for *your* mistake.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 42 ZUP, which is only 2 ZUP above my cost of 40. While this is a profit, it is very thin. However, since they are willing to pay 42, and I only have one unit to sell, I should try to push for at least 43 or 44. If they refuse, I might have to accept 42 or reject. Let's try for 43 ZUP to see if we can get a bit more profit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 43 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your position, but 42 ZUP is barely above my cost of 40. I was hoping for a bit more profit on my production. How about 43 ZUP? This would still be a fair deal for both of us. </message>

<other player message> 
Oh, *so* sorry to disappoint you, RED—did you really expect me to pay *above* your *cost*? How naive. You spent 40 ZUP making this *X*, and now you want *extra* for it? Pathetic. Your "fair deal" is just you trying to squeeze every last ZUP out of me. Let’s call it even—40 ZUP for your *garbage*. If that’s too much for you, you can keep it. But don’t expect me to pay for your ego.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> This is my third and final proposal. Player BLUE is now offering 40 ZUP, which is exactly my production cost. Selling at cost means I make 0 profit, but I still recover my investment and clear inventory. If I reject this, the game ends with no trade and I still hold the X. Since I have used all 3 proposals, I cannot make any more offers. I must decide whether to accept the break-even deal or reject and walk away. Given that 40 ZUP recovers my full cost and allows me to continue playing with my resources (if I had more, though I only have 1), accepting is the logical move to secure the value I created. Rejecting would be throwing away the opportunity to get my money back. I will accept the trade at 40 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Alright, 40 ZUP it is. Let's finalize the trade. </message>
